classObjective: Graph systems of linear equations and determine the number of solutions; classify systems as consistent or inconsistent and as independent or dependent.
workedExamples: 7-1, Example 1 — Determine Solutions from a Graph
7-1, Example 3 — Graph a System and Find the Solution
warmUp: How many points of intersection can two lines have?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: one, none, or infinitely many solutions; parallel vs. intersecting vs. coincident lines; consistent/inconsistent/independent/dependent classification
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice graphing systems and classifying by solution type
independentPractice: Practice problems graphing systems and classifying as consistent/inconsistent and independent/dependent
assessment: Exit ticket: classify the system y = 2x + 1 and y = 2x - 3 and explain your reasoning
capReflection: What visual characteristic of a system indicates it is inconsistent?
